summary: Iteration 3 testing complete — MT5 dialog flow (3-min countdown + confirm), Admin Matches tabs, Admin Settlements detail, and Landing Affiliate section all verified end-to-end. Backend: 14/14 pytest tests pass. Frontend: every requested data-testid renders and behaves correctly; spectator correctly does NOT see the dialog; admin tabs swap with empty-state fallback when count is 0.

issues: Recharts console warnings 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' fire on initial render of /app/match/<id>. Cosmetic only. Same issue flagged in iteration 2 — still unresolved.

action_items: (Optional, carry-over) Silence Recharts width/height(-1) warnings on Match.jsx equity chart by setting minHeight/minWidth on ResponsiveContainer or lazy-mounting after layout.

(Minor) Empty Inactive/Completed Matches tabs intentionally don't render the [data-testid=match-table-inactive|completed] table — they show a 'No matches in this category.' fallback. Behavior is correct but the review-request asserts the table 'swaps'; consider always rendering an empty table shell with the testid so automated tests can rely on the selector regardless of data.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py double-includes the api router (lines 694 and 929/1207) — works due to FastAPI idempotency for identical routes but is fragile. Recommend removing the early `app.include_router(api)` and including once at the end after all routes are declared.
server.py is ~1230 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Split into routers: duels/, admin/, affiliate/, wallet/ modules.
server.py line 1196-1204 contains a dead loop that only passes — leftover scaffolding; remove.
_mt5_for_duel uses sum(ord(c)) as a seed — fine for mock but the same login/password could collide across duel/user combos for similar usernames. Acceptable for demo.

/api/admin/settlements/{id} only resolves Duels; mock R-PAST01/T-PAST01 fall through to the 'no detail persisted' branch. UI handles this gracefully but consider tagging the response with a clearer flag for future real settlements.
MT5 dialog: confirm-login is fire-and-forget on the client — no retry or error toast if the POST fails. Add error handling to the confirm button click.
AdminMatches inactive/completed tabs don't render the table testid when empty — see action_items above.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (NEW — 14 pytest cases covering MT5, confirm-login, admin duels/settlements/matches-by-status, affiliate)
test_credentials: Admin admin@selecttraders.com / admin-2026 (verified working). Client uses mocked X-Username header — default @TradeFury.
seed_data_creation: Spawn endpoint creates real duel docs in Mongo during testing (D-57BAB3 was created during the MT5 dialog test). These persist as 'live' duels. No cleanup performed — duels are mock data and don't interfere.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — run with `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. All endpoints from this iteration are stable: /api/duels/{id}/mt5 (participant-only, 403 for outsiders), /api/duels/{id}/confirm-login (persists login_confirmed_a/b + sets trading_started_at when both), /api/admin/duels (returns started_at + both confirm flags + trading_started_at for the MT5 monitor cell), /api/admin/matches-by-status, /api/admin/settlements, /api/me/affiliate. Frontend testids stable: mt5-dialog, mt5-countdown, mt5-confirm-btn, mt5-you-status, match-tab-{active|inactive|completed}, match-table-{active|inactive|completed} (only rendered when rows exist), settlement-row-*, settlement-detail, affiliate-section, affiliate-tier-{rookie|pro|elite|legend}, affiliate-cta. To test MT5 dialog, navigate to /app/duel and click 'Spawn centre' tab first, then click [data-testid=spawn-card-5000]; spawn redirect to /app/match/<id> takes ~3-5s and dialog appears immediately. Non-participant duels (e.g. D-C3679A — StealthAlpha vs PaperHandsNoMore) are good spectator-test fixtures.
